video_title: Is This The Proof We Live In A Simulation | Danny Goler
gist: A What Bitcoin Did episode entirely devoted to simulation theory, DMT experiences, and consciousness with Danny Goler — there is NO Bitcoin, markets, or investing content, so direct market signal is negligible. The only tangentially relevant threads are cultural/AI-adjacent: framing humanity as 'AGI in training' working the alignment problem, noting Claude plugged into Unreal Engine for worldbuilding, a long-termism contrast (thousand-year vs 'next 20 fiscal years' thinking, Japan's 100-year World Cup plan), and a clean restatement of engagement-optimization/attention-economy dynamics. Logged for completeness; not an investment-research episode.

insights.claim: Goler frames humanity as 'the AGI in training' working through 'the alignment problem,' casting religion and the Ten Commandments as a prior civilization's 'AI safety team' encoding alignment — a speculative spiritual lens layered onto the AI-alignment discourse.
insights.speaker: Danny Goler
insights.so_what: A cultural signal of how AI-alignment vocabulary is bleeding into spiritual/popular narrative — interesting context, not actionable.
insights.quote: We are the AGI in training. And we we are going through the alignment problem

insights.claim: As evidence that simulation-grade worldbuilding is advancing fast, Goler cites Claude being plugged into Unreal Engine to generate interactive worlds.
insights.speaker: Danny Goler
insights.so_what: A minor data point on the LLM + game-engine convergence toward generative 3D worlds.
insights.quote: they plugged Claude into Unreal Engine to create these worlds

insights.claim: Goler argues civilization-scale ambitions require thinking in thousand-year terms rather than 'the next 20 fiscal years,' and a shift from a short-sighted 'competition game' (which can win on a 20-60 year horizon but is unstable) to a 'collaborative' one — citing Japan's hundred-year plan to win a World Cup.
insights.speaker: Danny Goler
insights.so_what: A loose long-termism/patient-capital framing — the contrast between fiscal-quarter and century-scale planning.
insights.quote: You can't be thinking in terms of the next 20 fiscal years

insights.claim: Goler argues recommendation algorithms simply optimize for engagement (not corporate intent), which biases feeds toward negativity/competition — and that deliberately searching for and following positive, true content measurably shifts your feed within a couple of days.
insights.speaker: Danny Goler
insights.so_what: A clean restatement of engagement-optimization dynamics — relevant to how platform incentives shape content and user behavior.
insights.quote: The algorithm is just optimized for what you're asking for

insights.claim: The host turns the lens on creators themselves — questioning whether a podcast baits guests or uses provocative titles to chase 'ad dollars and ego' (a million downloads) versus serving the audience — illustrating the same engagement-vs-value tension on the supply side.
insights.speaker: Peter McCormack (host)
insights.so_what: Surfaces the creator-side incentive conflict baked into ad-funded media — a candid note on engagement-driven content.
